title: Historical trend view per system (sample periods over time)
context: Users may benefit from seeing how a water system's contaminant measurements have changed across multiple sample periods — whether violations have resolved, whether detected levels have trended. This is informational only, using the existing past public record. The feature must carry the standard no-verdict + limitations framing and must never imply prediction, extrapolation, or a future-safety assessment. No new data is generated; only existing snapshot data is visualized across sample periods.
objective: Build a historical trend view per water system in the web app that displays sample-period time series from the normalized snapshot for core contaminants: (1) uses only past public record from existing snapshots; (2) carries the standard limitations/caveats (data lags, detection limits, sampling cadence, gaps); (3) never implies prediction, trend extrapolation, or a future-safety assessment; (4) carries no safety verdict or grade; (5) is accessible (WCAG 2.2 AA).
acceptanceCriteria: Historical trend view displays contaminant measurement data across multiple sample periods from existing snapshots
View uses only past public record; no prediction, extrapolation, or future-safety assessment is implied or displayed
View carries the standard limitations caveats: data lags, detection limits, sampling cadence, coverage gaps
No safety verdict, grade, or ranking emitted in the historical view
View meets WCAG 2.2 AA; zero critical axe violations
pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass
output: PR adding historical trend view per water system with limitations/caveats and no-verdict framing.
